name: Bayesian Belief-Based Foraging with Gut Signals as Priors
description: Probabilistic decision framework where gut-derived hormonal signals (a composite satiety signal and ghrelin) shape Bayesian posterior beliefs about the agent's latent hunger state. Action values blend homeostatic need with hedonic reward evaluation, and actions are selected via softmax. Inspired by Mayer's (2011) interoceptive 'gut feelings' framework and active inference ideas, with hormonal dynamics from MDPI Nutrients (2023) and PMC11483575 (2024).

variables.name: gut_hormone_composite
variables.description: Aggregated anorexigenic gut signal (composite of GLP-1, PYY, CCK). Decays over time, boosted by eating.

variables.name: ghrelin_signal
variables.description: Orexigenic interoceptive ghrelin signal. Rises during fasting, suppressed by eating.

variables.name: hunger_belief
variables.description: Bayesian posterior probability that the agent is in a 'hungry' state, in [0,1]

variables.name: value_eat
variables.description: Expected value of eating at current position, blending homeostatic need and hedonic reward

variables.name: value_move
variables.description: Expected value of moving in direction d, based on hunger belief and food proximity

variables.name: value_stay
variables.description: Expected value of staying in place (higher when sated)

variables.name: ate_observation
variables.description: Binary observation: did the agent successfully eat last tick

parameters.name: hunger_drift_rate
parameters.source: Yoo & Park (2021): tonic ghrelin rise rate during fasting

parameters.name: satiety_impulse
parameters.source: Berthoud (2011): post-meal satiety peptide surge magnitude

parameters.name: interoceptive_noise
parameters.source: Mayer (2011): interoceptive variability in gut-to-brain signaling

parameters.name: expected_ghrelin_when_hungry
parameters.source: MDPI Nutrients (2023): reference ghrelin level in fasted state

parameters.name: expected_satiety_when_sated
parameters.source: Berthoud (2011): reference satiety hormone level post-meal

rules.description: Gut hormone composite update: decays exponentially, boosted by eating
rules.pseudocode:
h_gut = (1.0 - tau_gut) * h_gut + lambda_satiety * o_ate
h_gut = max(h_gut, 0.0)

rules.description: Ghrelin signal update: rises during fasting, suppressed by eating
rules.pseudocode:
g = g + lambda_hunger * (1.0 - o_ate) - 0.5 * g * o_ate
g = max(g, 0.0)

rules.description: Bayesian likelihood of being hungry given ghrelin observation
Bayesian likelihood of being sated given gut hormone observation

rules.pseudocode:
numerator = ell_H * b_H
denominator = ell_H * b_H + ell_S * (1.0 - b_H)
b_H = numerator / denominator if denominator > 1e-10 else 0.5

rules.description: Expected value of eating: blends homeostatic hunger belief with hedonic component attenuated by satiety
rules.pseudocode: V_eat = omega_homeo * b_H * R_food + omega_hedon * R_food * (1.0 / (1.0 + h_gut))

rules.description: Expected value of moving in direction d: scales with hunger belief and food proximity in that direction
rules.pseudocode:
# For each direction d in [move_up, move_down, move_left, move_right]:
# Compute position after moving in d
# Find nearest food from that new position
# food_proximity_d = 1.0 / (1.0 + manhattan_distance_to_nearest_food_from_new_pos)
V_move_d = b_H * food_proximity_d * R_food - C_move

rules.description: Expected value of staying: rewarded when sated (no need to move)

decision_logic.description: The agent updates gut signals and Bayesian hunger belief, computes action values for all available actions, then selects an action via softmax probability distribution over the action values. The eat action is only considered if food is at the agent's current position.
pseudocode: # 1. Update gut signals from last tick's outcome
apply_rules(R1, R2)  # update h_gut and g based on o_ate
apply_rules(R3a, R3b, R4)  # compute likelihoods and posterior b_H
food_here = any(f for f in perception.resources.food if f.x == perception.x and f.y == perception.y)
apply_rule(R6)  # compute V_move_d for direction d
exp_values = {a: exp(v / T) for a, v in action_values.items()}
probs = {a: ev / total for a, ev in exp_values.items()}

perception_to_variables.o_ate: 1 if last action was 'eat' and reward > 0, else 0

env_mapping.reward_source: eat action → ConsumeEffect reward of 1.0; used to set o_ate for next tick

expected_behaviors.description: Hunger belief rises during fasting as ghrelin increases toward expected hungry level
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: run 50 steps without eating → assert b_H approaches 1.0 and g approaches g_exp_hungry

expected_behaviors.description: Eating causes satiety surge that shifts belief toward sated state
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: agent eats → assert h_gut increases → next tick b_H decreases significantly

expected_behaviors.description: Agent probabilistically prefers stay when fully sated (b_H near 0)
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: set h_gut high, g low → assert P(stay) is highest among action probabilities

expected_behaviors.description: Hedonic component allows some eating even when mildly sated, if food is present
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: b_H = 0.3, food at position → assert V_eat > 0 due to omega_hedon term

expected_behaviors.description: Movement toward food is preferred when hungry and food is visible but distant
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: b_H = 0.9, food 5 cells away → assert V_move toward food > V_stay

references: Mayer, E.A. (2011) — Gut feelings: the emerging biology of gut-brain communication — Neuropharmacology / ScienceDirect
Berthoud, H.R. (2011) — The role of the gut/brain axis in modulating food intake — Neuropharmacology
Yoo, J.Y. & Park, S. (2021) — Role of the gut-brain axis in energy and glucose metabolism — npj Science of Food / Nature
MDPI Nutrients (2023) — The Mechanism of the Gut-Brain Axis in Regulating Food Intake — Nutrients 15(17):3728 (PubMed 37686760)
Pharmacology Research & Perspectives (2024) — The gut-brain axis in appetite, satiety, food intake, and eating disorders — PMC11483575
